description: Train an IMAGE-ONLY LTX-2.3 style / identity LoRA on Spark Fuse with the official Lightricks ltx-trainer. Feed a folder of stills + a trigger word; you get back a stackable .safetensors LoRA in ShareSync that drops straight into comfy_spark's --lora. This is the easiest, most reliable kind to train first (no motion to learn) — see README 'Training your own LoRA'. Motion / IC-LoRA recipes come later (kind: video / ic-lora).
_TEMPLATE: Both base weights are FILLED — no placeholders, runs as-is (modulo the first-real-run validation tracked in EZ_COMFY_TODO.md). The 'dev' checkpoint is the single .safetensors the trainer's --model-path wants. The gemma encoder is NOT the single ComfyUI file the generation presets use: the ltx-trainer's --text-encoder-path wants a full gemma transformers MODEL DIRECTORY (verified against the trainer's configuration-reference: 'Path to the Gemma text encoder model directory'). google/gemma-3-12b-it is GATED, so this recipe points at the UNGATED unsloth/gemma-3-12b-it mirror (same weights, no token), snapshot-downloaded on the node. To use the canonical gated Google repo instead, set the gemma entry's hf_repo to google/gemma-3-12b-it and pass --hf-token. Validate cheaply first with --dry-run (prints the dataset.json + resolved YAML + the exact container command sequence, spends nothing).
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path mirroring comfy_spark: a public CUDA-13 base image runs lora_train_run.py, which clones Lightricks/LTX-2, 'uv sync's the ltx-trainer env, downloads the base weights onto the node, (optionally) auto-captions any un-captioned image, runs scripts/process_dataset.py to cache latents, writes a YAML from this recipe, runs scripts/train.py, then self-uploads the trained LoRA to the job's ShareSync folder. Heavy cold start (weights + uv env); use --idle-hold to keep a warm node across iterations.

prompts: a portrait, soft natural light, shallow depth of field
a wide landscape at golden hour

models.note: Ungated mirror of google/gemma-3-12b-it (no HF license/token needed). The ltx-trainer --text-encoder-path wants this whole MODEL DIRECTORY, so it's snapshot-downloaded on the node. To use the canonical gated Google repo, set hf_repo to google/gemma-3-12b-it and pass --hf-token.

ui.title: Train an LTX-2.3 style / identity LoRA (image-only)

primary_input.label: Dataset folder (images)
primary_input.tooltip: A folder of 20-50 stills for a style/look, or 30-100 for a specific face/object. Optional same-named .txt files become captions; anything without one is auto-captioned on the node. Give it a distinctive --trigger word (e.g. 'rax_style').

ui.label: Rank
ui.tooltip: LoRA capacity. 32 is the LTX-2.3 default and the right starting point. Higher = more capacity but bigger files + more overfitting risk; lower generalizes better. Stay in 16-64 for concept/style work.

ui.label: Training steps
ui.tooltip: How long to train. Validate previews around 500-750 steps; beyond ~1000 watch for overfitting (LoRA reproduces your data verbatim and ignores prompts). Default 2000.

ui.label: Low-VRAM mode (48GB cards)
ui.tooltip: Enables INT8 quantization + 8-bit text encoder so training fits a 48GB (l40s) card. Off = the comfortable 80GB+ path (rtxpro6000).
